fields.description:
# WS3 — Escalation scripted ops + the code-review workflow

## Why
This is the heart of the gate: wire WS1's base + WS2's overlays into the two-round overlay-escalation topology and feed all findings into the shipped kernel Pass-2/3/4. The mechanism was DERISKED on the real v3 engine (offline) — the agent's `recommend_overlays` drove a second batch's membership, bogus ids dropped, the cap bounded fan-out, one-hop held — so this is implementation of a proven design, with NO core-engine change.

## What
- Two deterministic scripted ops: `overlay_union` (compute (glob U base.recommend) - already-run, ONE-HOP, capped at N=3 configurable; emit per-overlay `include_<overlay>` flags + `to_run`) and `merge_findings` (concatenate base + Round-A + Round-B finding lists).
- The thin `gates/code-review.yaml`: assemble_diff -> triggers (overlay_triggers) -> base (agent) -> round_a (batch, when:=triggers) -> union -> round_b (batch, when:=union) -> merge -> verify (Pass-2) -> decide (Pass-3) -> coach_gate/coach (Pass-4).

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py` (or register in `workflow/steps.py`) — `overlay_union`, `merge_findings`.
- `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml` — the gate workflow.
- OUT OF SCOPE: the `gate_dispatch` entry + source-separation (WS4); the secrets/security auto-block detectors (WS5).

## Specifics pinned (plan-review remediation 2026-06)
- **Escalation cannot affect the verdict (explicit invariant):** the offline test asserts that flipping `recommend_overlays` changes only WHICH advisory overlays run (Round-B membership), never the Pass-3 decision for an identical finding set — escalation lives on the recall/selection side; the precision/decision side is deterministic.
- **`overlay_union` config:** the cap N (default 3) is read from `gates/code-review.yaml`, not hard-coded; `merge_findings` preserves per-finding provenance (which reviewer/round emitted it) so Pass-2 can re-ground each.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `overlay_union` computes (glob U recommend) - already-run, ONE-HOP, capped at N=3 (configurable), enum-constrained; `merge_findings` concatenates the three finding sources.
- [ ] `gates/code-review.yaml` validates (`rebar workflow validate`) and runs on the v3 engine; Round-A overlays are glob-triggered, Round-B membership = the escalated union, all findings flow into kernel Pass-2/3/4.
- [ ] An OFFLINE (FakeRunner) test asserts: Round-B membership == the escalated set; the cap bounds fan-out; one-hop holds (overlays do not re-escalate); escalation can never change the deterministic verdict.
- [ ] The gate calls the kernel `verify_findings` / `pass3_over_findings` / `coach` directly (no forked passes).
